topic_title: Iterating a specification based on output gaps
verdict: Corpus passes all six checks. All required sections are present and non-trivial. Citation density meets the web_search strict standard: primary resource [1] cited 10+ times; all three resources cited throughout §5–§7; no fake citations. No forward references beyond 2.9's own concepts and prior-topic vocabulary. Word count (~2966w) is marginally below the 3000w standard-profile floor but well within the 30% tolerance band (floor would need to drop below 2100w to hard-fail); soft note logged. Scope stays cleanly on iterating/patching specs — the three-question verification check from 2.8 is referenced as a tool (not re-taught), and no advanced or adjacent concepts are introduced at depth. Beginner readability is good: all non-everyday terms defined on first use, no jargon dumps, plain register throughout.

citation_density.estimated_coverage: all standalone factual assertions in §5/§6/§7 carry at least one citation marker

sequence_drift.notes: All technical terms are either introduced in this topic (iteration, gap, iteration cycle, gap-to-fix mapping, minimum viable patch, accept with known limitation) or defined in prior topics 2.1–2.8. The REFINE framework (§7) is a named external model cited from resource [3], introduced in one paragraph with plain-language expansion — not a new curriculum concept.

findings.detail: Estimated word count ~2966 is ~34 words below the 3000w standard-profile floor. Within the 30% tolerance band (hard-fail threshold: 2100w). Corpus is substantive; borderline length does not impede coverage.

topic_scope.notes: Corpus stays on the iteration/patching headline throughout. §6 Implementation and §7 Real-World Patterns reinforce rather than extend the topic. Testing vocabulary from 2.8 (three-question verification check, pass/fail table) is referenced as prior knowledge only, not re-taught.

beginner_readability.notes: All introduced terms defined on first use with bold labels and plain-language definitions. REFINE acronym expanded inline at point of introduction (§7). No paragraph introduces 3+ new terms simultaneously. Sentence length is short to medium throughout. No hedging phrases detected.
